title: Is DexCom (DXCM) Fairly Priced After Recent Share Price Weakness?
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

If you are wondering whether DexCom's current share price lines up with its underlying worth, or if the market is mispricing the stock, this article walks through that question step by step. DexCom recently closed at US$62.22, with returns of 5.9% decline over 7 days, 14.9% decline over 30 days, 6.5% decline year to date, 0.7% over 1 year, 44.7% decline over 3 years and 34.2% decline over 5 years. These moves can signal shifting views on both opportunity and risk. These mixed returns have come as DexCom continues to attract attention as a key player in medical technology. Shifts in sentiment can quickly affect how investors think about what they are willing to pay for the shares. Understanding what has recently influenced expectations around the business can help you judge whether the current price reflects caution, optimism or something in between. On Simply Wall St's valuation framework, DexCom has a value score of 3 out of 6. The rest of this article looks at how different valuation methods line up with that result, and then finishes with a broader way to think about value that goes beyond the usual ratios and models.

DexCom delivered 0.7% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Medical Equipment industry.

Approach 1: DexCom Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required return.

For DexCom, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.08b. Analysts provide free cash flow estimates out to 2028, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates beyond that, with projected free cash flow of $1.66b in 2030. For each year from 2026 through 2035, those future cash flows are discounted back to today in dollar terms and summed.

On this basis, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $63.57 per share, compared with the recent share price of $62.22. That implies the stock is around 2.1% undervalued, which is a very small gap and suggests the market price and the model estimate are broadly aligned.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

DexCom is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.DXCM Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for DexCom.

Story Continues

Approach 2: DexCom Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like DexCom, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for what investors are currently willing to pay for each dollar of earnings. It links directly to the bottom line, which is ultimately what supports long term returns.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on what investors expect from a business and how risky they think those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty usually lines up with a lower one.

DexCom currently trades on a P/E of 28.63x. That sits above the Medical Equipment industry average of 27.43x and also above the peer group average of 23.19x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates that, given DexCom’s characteristics, a P/E of 29.41x would be more in line with its earnings profile. This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than a simple industry or peer comparison because it adjusts for factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, risk profile, industry and market cap in one combined view.

With a current P/E of 28.63x versus a Fair Ratio of 29.41x, DexCom’s valuation by this metric appears about in line with what the model suggests.

Result: ABOUT RIGHTNasdaqGS:DXCM P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your DexCom Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives help you turn your view of DexCom into a clear story that links what you think about its products, competition and risks to a set of numbers on future revenue, earnings, margins and a fair value. All of this is available in an easy tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page that compares your Fair Value to the current price, updates automatically as new news or earnings arrive, and can range from a more cautious view closer to US$68 to a more optimistic stance nearer US$112, depending on how you interpret the same information.

Do you think there's more to the story for DexCom? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!NasdaqGS:DXCM 1-Year Stock Price Chart

title: Dexcom Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call for April 30, 2026 at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time
content:
SAN DIEGO, April 02, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--DexCom, Inc. (NASDAQ:DXCM) today announced that it plans to release its first quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Thursday, April 30, 2026. Management will hold a conference call to review the company's first quarter 2026 performance starting at 4:30 p.m. (Eastern Time) on the same day. The conference call will be concurrently webcast. The link to the webcast will be available on the Dexcom investor relations website at investors.dexcom.com and will be archived there for future reference.

To listen to the conference call, please dial (888) 414-4585 (US/Canada) or (646) 960-0331 (International) and use the confirmation ID "9430114" approximately five minutes prior to the start time.

About DexCom, Inc.

Dexcom empowers people to take control of health through innovative biosensing technology. Founded in 1999, Dexcom has pioneered and set the standard in glucose biosensing for more than 25 years. Its technology has transformed how people manage diabetes and track their glucose, helping them feel more in control and live more confidently.

Category: IR
